channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Video published by Ukraine allegedly shows North Korean soldiers in Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A video purporting to show dozens of North Korean recruits lining up to collect Russian military fatigues has been released by Ukrainian officials, who say it shows the introduction of troops sent by Pyongyang into the conflict.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guatemalan judge grants investigative journalist Zamora house arrest and his family celebrates
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — The family of a Guatemalan journalist on Saturday celebrated his transfer to house arrest following his jailing for more than two years amid his daily newspaper's anti-corruption investigations.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PEREZ D., Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Strike on Netanyahu home 'a signal' Iran proxies not incapacitated, expert says
item.description: A recent drone strike targeting the home of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is sending "a signal" that Iran-backed militant groups are not "incapacitated," despite losing senior leadership in recent weeks, according to a Middle East expert. Daniel Levy, the president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: Cuba’s government says some electricity has been restored on the island after one of the country’s major power plants failed and left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea says it discovered the remains of a South Korean drone in Pyongyang
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Saturday that authorities found the remains of a South Korean drone during a search in the capital, Pyongyang, and that the drone proved that the South’s military was behind the alleged drone infiltrations over the city’s skies earlier in the month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hezbollah fires rocket salvo at northern Israel, Iranian leader vows 'Axis of Resistance' will continue
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces said at least 180 projectiles were fired into Israel from Lebanon as Hezbollah ramps up its attacks on the Jewish state with backing from Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harvard donations drop; alums cut ties over anti-Israel protests
item.description: Harvard, the nation's richest university, saw donations plunge 15% after campus protests, new report shows.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, The Hill

item.title: The first migrants sent to Albania for processing return to Italy
item.description: An Italian navy ship has taken back to Italy the first 12 migrants from newly opened asylum processing centers in Albania following a highly-debated court decision in Rome.
item.dc:creator: Vlasov Sulaj And Llazar Semini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A drone targets the Israeli prime minister's house while strikes in Gaza kill more than 50
item.description: Israel’s government says a drone targeted Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s house on Saturday with no casualties.
item.dc:creator: Adam Schreck And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexican newspaper offices hit by gunfire in Sinaloa state capital
item.description: A dozen gunshots were fired at a building housing the Mexican newspaper El Debate in the northern state of Sinaloa; the newspaper found at least 4 bullet impacts on the walls of the building.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN expands arms embargo on Haiti to all types of arms and ammunition
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. Security Council voted unanimously Friday to expand the arms embargo in Haiti to all types of weapons and ammunition, expressing grave concern at the extremely high levels of gang violence and criminal activities in the impoverished Caribbean nation.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cuban power plant failure leaves millions in the dark in island-wide outage
item.description: Millions of Cubans were left without power on Friday after a failure at a power plant, the communist government said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the Uluburun shipwreck was discovered accidentally by a sponge diver
item.description: The Uluburun shipwreck was accidently discovered by a sponge diver in 1982. Following the discovery, excavation of the area took archaeologists a decade to complete.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah vows a new phase in the war, as Israel says it has killed Hamas leader Sinwar
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Lebanese militant group Hezbollah vowed to launch a new phase of fighting against invading Israeli troops Friday, a day after Israel said its forces in the Palestinian enclave of Gaza had killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, the chief architect of last year’s brutal attack on Israe
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Military voters see 'direct attack' from GOP election challenges
item.description: Republicans are coming under fire from veterans and military groups for attempting to restrict the votes of overseas service members through a series of lawsuits in battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Russia flaunts its many doomsday weapons to keep the West from ramping up support for Ukraine
item.description: This year has seen President Vladimir Putin repeatedly brandish the nuclear sword, reminding everyone that Russia has the world’s largest atomic arsenal to try to deter the West from ramping up support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel says it has killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli forces in Gaza killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year’s attack on Israel that sparked the war, the military said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Argentine authorities probe what happened before Liam Payne's fatal fall from his hotel balcony
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — The circumstances surrounding the death of ex-One Direction singer Liam Payne were suspicious and possibly involved drugs, though there was no sign of a third party being involved, Argentine prosecutors said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kim stresses to North Korean troops they should treat South Korea as a hostile foreign enemy
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un reminded his troops to treat South Korea as a hostile foreign enemy and insisted that the North wouldn’t hesitate to attack its rival if the South infringes upon its sovereignty, state media said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sinwar's removal from battlefield is ‘significant,’ presents opportunity for cease-fire, State Department says
item.description: The State Department on Thursday said the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar presents an "opportunity" for a potential cease-fire and an end to the yearlong war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas leader’s death shakes up Middle East war
item.description: The death of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar has shaken up the war in the Middle East, with Israel now having eliminated the top leaders of the Palestinian militant group, including the officials who plotted the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Hamas terror leader Yahya Sinwar killed in Gaza, Israel says
item.description: Israeli officials announced Thursday that Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar was killed during military operations inside the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: South Korea condemned the North's constitutional revision as “an anti-unification, anti-national act.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel pressured by UN and US to step up action to tackle Gaza's escalating humanitarian crisis
item.description: Israel’s U.N. Ambassador Danny Danon insisted that his country’s humanitarian efforts remain “as comprehensive as ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy outlines his 'victory plan' to Ukraine's lawmakers, including a call to join NATO
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy told lawmakers Wednesday that Ukraine’s Western partners are increasing pressure to negotiate with Russia, but he hinted such talks would be unfavorable to Kyiv as he unveiled what he called his “victory plan” for the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A Christmas Island National Park travel guide where millions of colorful crabs migrate annually
item.description: From diving to birdwatching, there is a plethora of experiences to take part in at Christmas Island National Park. The red crab migration is one popular experience for tourists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 21 people
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 21 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. The Israeli military did not immediately comment and the target was not clear.
item.dc:creator: YAHYA HABCHITI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vial linked to UK woman's death had enough poison to kill thousands
item.description: Sturgess and her partner collapsed after they came into contact with a discarded perfume bottle containing the nerve agent.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
